content.problemStatement: Write a program to check whether a given year is a leap year or not. If a number is divisible by 400400400 or divisible by 444 but not by 100100100, then the year is considered a leap year. Print **Leap year** if the given year is a leap year; otherwise, print **Not a leap year**.
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer year.
content.outputFormat: For each test case print Leap year if the year satisfies the leap‑year condition, otherwise print Not a leap year. Each answer on its own line.

sampleTestCases.output:
Leap year
Not a leap year
Leap year
sampleTestCases.explanation: 2024 is divisible by 4 and not by 100 → Leap year. 2023 satisfies neither condition → Not a leap year. 2000 is divisible by 400 → Leap year.
1900 is divisible by 100 but not by 400, so it is not a leap year.

sampleTestCases.output:
Leap year
Not a leap year
sampleTestCases.explanation: 4 is divisible by 4 and not by 100 → Leap year. 100 is divisible by 100 but not by 400 → Not a leap year.

hidden.label: maximum boundary
hidden.expectedOutput:
Leap year
Not a leap year

hidden.expectedOutput:
Leap year
Leap year
Leap year

hidden.expectedOutput:
Not a leap year
Leap year
Not a leap year

Not a leap year
Leap year
Not a leap year
Leap year
Not a leap year

solution.approach: A year is a leap year if it is divisible by 400 or if it is divisible by 4 but not by 100. For each test case we simply evaluate these two conditions using the modulo operator. The check is O(1) per test case, so the whole solution runs in O(T) time.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case

code.C:
#include <stdio.h>
int main(){int T;if(scanf("%d",&T)!=1)return 0;while(T--){long long year;scanf("%lld",&year);if(year%400==0||(year%4==0&&year%100!=0))printf("Leap year\n");else printf("Not a leap year\n");}return 0;}
